$comment: Hand-authored named fact-probes for the capability engine. Each probe asserts the EXACT structural fields Slice 2 will map (basename resolution, wrapper peel, scope, amplifier flags, payload opacity, substitution extraction, parse_confidence). Slice 1 keeps kind=NONE (the capability pass is a stub) — these probes prove the facts are not merely LIVE but OPERATIONALLY SUFFICIENT for Slice 2 to consume. Assertions read the structured CommandFact emitted on STDERR under CAWS_CLASSIFY_FACTS_DUMP=1, never the human reason prose. `expect` lists only the fields the probe checks; absent fields are not asserted. `expect_decision` is the Slice-1 (unchanged) stdout decision — included to prove zero decision change at the probe surface too.

probes.name: basename-resolution-absolute-path
probes.note: absolute path resolves to basename su (su is the executable, not a wrapper); sudo/su/doas engine rule marks PRIV_ESC even in Slice 1 (engine rule, not adapter)

probes.name: wrapper-peel-env-and-sudo
probes.command: env FOO=bar sudo kubectl get pods
probes.note: env assignment + sudo peeled; the real executable kubectl is surfaced for Slice 2 adapter lookup

probes.name: prod-scope-inference
probes.note: production token -> scope=prod; --all captured. Slice 2 will map this to deny via the lattice

probes.name: dangerous-verb-position-subcommand
probes.note: destroy verb captured in subcommand_path for Slice 2 IAC_DESTROY mapping

probes.name: amplifier-flag-volumes
probes.note: --volumes amplifier captured; Slice 2 raises reversibility to irreversible

probes.name: quoted-literal-payload-opaque-exec
probes.note: eval with a LITERAL payload -> opacity=literal. Slice 2 will recurse-classify the payload to deny. (Slice 1 still allows — the pass is a stub.)

probes.name: opaque-payload-marker
probes.note: eval with a $VAR payload -> opacity=opaque. Slice 2 maps opaque exec to ask (cannot prove payload).

probes.name: command-substitution-extraction
probes.note: the $(...) body is extracted and recursively classified; rm -rf /etc -> ask at baseline (verified: /etc is not repo-root/home, so classify_rm_target returns ask, not deny). Existing machinery, NOT the capability pass. Proves substitution recursion is intact and the decision is unchanged from baseline.

probes.name: parse-confidence-low-unparseable
probes.note: shlex cannot tokenize an unterminated quote -> parse_confidence low; the fact degrades gracefully (no crash) and the decision is unchanged from baseline

probes.name: shred-flag
probes.note: shred executable + -u captured; Slice 2 maps to FS_SHRED (irreversible)

probes.name: DEEP-NEST-KNOWN-GAP
probes.note: KNOWN GAP (carried from Slice 0): deeply-escaped sh -c nesting returns allow == baseline because the nested-shell unwrap stops re-expanding before the recursion budget engages. This is NOT closed by the budget; it tests whether Slice 3 opaque-exec recursion REPLACES the brittle escaped-quote unwrap. Captured as data so it cannot be lost to prose. Slice 1 asserts only the current (allow) behavior; Slice 3 flips expected to deny.
